title: Finding time using $s = d \div t$
problem: A train travels 350 km at a constant speed of 70 km/h. How long does the journey take?

title: Converting km/h to m/s
problem: A car travels at 108 km/h. Convert this speed to m/s.

steps.working: 1 km = 1000 m and 1 hour = 3600 s, so to convert km/h to m/s, multiply by $\dfrac{1000}{3600} = \dfrac{5}{18}$.

title: Average speed for a return journey
problem: A car travels 240 km from city A to city B at 80 km/h, then returns along the same route at 60 km/h. Find the average speed for the entire journey.

steps.instruction: Find the time for each leg of the journey.
Find the total distance and total time.
Note: the average speed is not simply the average of 80 and 60.
steps.working: The average of 80 and 60 would give $\dfrac{80 + 60}{2} = 70$ km/h, but the car spends more time at the slower speed, so the true average is less than 70 km/h.
